translated: Jesus said to them, "I am the bread of life, he who comes to me will never be hungry, and he who believes in me will never be thirsty, but as I told you, you have seen me and yet you do not believe, all that the Father gives me will come to me, and whoever comes to me I will never drive away, for I have come down from heaven not to do my will but to do the will of him who sent me, and this is the will of him who sent me, that I shall lose none of all that he has given me, but raise them up at the last day, for my Father's will is that everyone who looks to the Son and believes in him shall have eternal life, and I will raise him up at the last day."

translated: Jesus and his disciples were in a place, and they said to him, "The crowd is pressing us and we have nothing to give them to eat. This place is remote and it is already getting late and we are hungry.

ref: You cannot be the slave of two masters! You will like one more than the other or be more loyal to one than the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Worry (Luke
text: “Nɛra kan ta'am tuna bɔ'ɔra kpɛ'ɛnduma bayi', se'ere en sɔi la, a wan ba'afum yaaɛ ayima gee nɔŋɛ ayima, bii a ba' tuna bɔ'ɔra ayima gee bahera ayima. Ya kan ta'am dɛna yameŋa bo' Yinɛ la lɔgerɔ buyina. Da fabele ya
translated: “When we are sitting here and the elderly are speaking, if someone is speaking and you want to respond, you should first ask permission from the elders before you speak, or you should let the elderly speak first before you speak. We are sitting under the shed of God's word today. Let us listen”

ref: If I use the power of Beelzebul to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? Your followers are the ones who will judge you.
text: Bala, mam han tara Beelezebule sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi, gee yamam nɛreba la me tari la ani sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi? Bala zuo, ya mɛhi zahereba la en wan gakɛ ya.
translated: No, my father is not Beelzebub's chief servant, how can his servants drive out demons? No, rather, someone stronger than him comes and overpowers him.

ref: Many false prophets will come and fool a lot of people.
text: Ti nɔtɔgeheba pumparenduma zo'e zo'e wan wa'am na pa'ahɛ nɛreba zo'e zo'e ti ba.
translated: The elders of old used to say that when you are going to a place, you should not tell everyone where you are going.

ref: A little while later some people standing there walked over to Peter and said, “We know you are one of them. We can tell it because you talk like someone from Galilee.”
text: Ti la iŋɛ fii wa ti nɛreba la n ze' la paaɛ Pita zi'an yele en yeti, “Yelemɛŋerɛ fum dela ba ayima. Fum tugum n pa'alɛ.”
translated: They went and told the chief's son that Peter's wife said, "The stranger's name is not good. The name is not pleasant."

ref: He then got into the boat with them, and the wind died down. The disciples were completely confused.
text: Gee nyaa zom uureŋɔ la zuo la ba, ti kusebego la ma'aɛ, ti la iŋɛ ba za'a pakerɛ.
translated: The chief of the village has gone to the farm, and the elder has gone to the market, and they left the children playing.

ref: Large crowds came and brought many people who were paralyzed or blind or lame or unable to talk. They placed them, and many others, in front of Jesus, and he healed them all.
text: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ kina a zi'an na, tara wɔbehi, kɔɔrenɔ, fɔɔhi, gɔɔnɔ, la basɛba zo'e zo'e, tari ba na gã'arɛ Yezu nɛŋan, ti a me tibɛ ba.
translated: They brought all kinds of animals, including cattle, sheep, goats, fowl, and all sorts of creatures, and presented them to Jesus' disciples, who gave them to the people.

ref: That evening, Jesus said to his disciples, “Let's cross to the east side.”
text: De daarɛ mɛa Zaanuurɛ, ti a yele a zahereba la yeti, “Bahɛ ya ti tu yakɛ ko'om wa yakenɛ deyima wa ha.”
translated: The elder of Zaare said to the young men, "Come, let us go and fetch water and bring it to our homes."

ref: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
text: Bala la, nɛra han ta yele fum yeti ‘Bihɛ, Kiresta n ŋwana,’ bii a boi la ke. De nam de.
translated: Come and see, the person is saying that Jesus is the Son of God, the thing is true.

ref: Jesus replied, “It isn't right to take food away from children and feed it to dogs.”
text: Ti Yezu lerege en yeti, “La dagi ma'a ti n dikɛ kɔma dia lobe bo' baahi.”
translated: Jesus looked at them and said, "It is written: 'Man shall not live on bread alone."

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.

text: A dele la Yinɛ. Yinɛ la yi faaɛ en bahɛ nananawa na, wuu a han bɔta, se'ere en sɔi la, a yeti eŋa dela Yinɛ Dayua.”
translated: They call him Yinɛ. Yinɛ is the one who has been given the land to rule over all, and when he is sitting, all the people come to him, and he says he is Yinɛ the ruler.

ref: The king will say to them, “Whenever you failed to help any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you failed to do it for me.”
text: Ti a nyaa wan lerege ba yeti, ‘N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, ti yamam n ka iŋɛ de bo' se'em en de bia mam suurɔ wa puan la, ya ka iŋɛ de bo' la mam n bala.’
translated: They said to the chief's son, 'We have found a certain woman, and we want you to go and take her as a wife for your father.'

ref: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, a yele a mɛŋa yeti, “Mam han pun kalum a fuo wa ma'a, n ban'an wa wan bahɛ.”
translated: They said among themselves, and one said to the other, "Let's go and dig a well for our mother, so she doesn't have to go far."

ref: As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he took his twelve disciples aside and told them in private:
text: Yezu en wa yɛ'a zɔna Yerusalɛm la, a doohe la a zahereba pia la bayi' la ma'a, ban ye' la, ti a yɛta ba sore la zuo yeti,
translated: Jesus and his disciples went to Jerusalem, and he entered the temple and began to drive out those who sold and those who bought in the temple, and he overturned the tables of the money-changers and the seats of those who sold pigeons.

ref: Simon Peter spoke up, “You are the Messiah, the Son of the living God.”
text: Ti Simɔn Pita lerege yeti, “Fum n de Kiresta la, Yinɛ vua la Dayua.”
translated: Simon Peter answered him, "You are the Christ, the Son of the living God."

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Hɛrɔd n ta baŋɛ ti baŋereba la pã'ahɛ en mɛ la, a ukɛ mɛ kankaŋi, gee bo' nuurɛ ti ba doohe kɔrum bibudibeto sɛba woo n boi Bɛtelehɛm la ka kɔrɔmkɔrɔm za'a, kɔmsɛba n de yuuma ayi' bii sɛba n pun ka paaɛ bala, doohe la hansɛka ti a wum ti baŋereba duma la tɔgɛ bo' en la.
translated: Herod was very angry when he realized that the wise men had tricked him, and he ordered the execution of all the male children in Bethlehem and its surroundings who were two years old and under, according to the time he had learned from the wise men.

ref: You cannot be the slave of two masters! You will like one more than the other or be more loyal to one than the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Worry (Luke
text: “Nɛra kan ta'am tuna bɔ'ɔra kpɛ'ɛnduma bayi', se'ere en sɔi la, a wan ba'afum yaaɛ ayima gee nɔŋɛ ayima, bii a ba' tuna bɔ'ɔra ayima gee bahera ayima. Ya kan ta'am dɛna yameŋa bo' Yinɛ la lɔgerɔ buyina. Da fabele ya
translated: “When we are sitting here and the elderly are speaking, if someone is speaking and you want to respond, you should first ask permission from the elders before you speak, or you should let the elderly speak first before you speak. We are sitting under the shade of God and the laws of the land are in our hands. The work is for all of us

ref: If I use the power of Beelzebul to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? Your followers are the ones who will judge you.
text: Bala, mam han tara Beelezebule sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi, gee yamam nɛreba la me tari la ani sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi? Bala zuo, ya mɛhi zahereba la en wan gakɛ ya.
translated: No, my father is not the lord of the flies, so how can I be the lord of the flies? No, friend, I am not worthy of that name.

ref: Many false prophets will come and fool a lot of people.
text: Ti nɔtɔgeheba pumparenduma zo'e zo'e wan wa'am na pa'ahɛ nɛreba zo'e zo'e ti ba.
